title: Psychedelic "brain boost" combinations for enhancing cellular energy production
abstract: Disclosed are therapeutic "brain boost" combinations of agents and pharmaceutical kits and compositions thereof, such as combinations of serotonergic (5-HT 2A receptor agonist) psychedelics, with mitochondrial energy enhancers, such as metabolic cofactors and metabolic coenzymes, in some embodiments together with antioxidants and glucose regulators. In some embodiments, the combinations are used in methods of enhancing energy, such as increasing cellular energy production or metabolism, and for optimizing glucose levels and protecting cells from oxidative damage. In some embodiments, the combinations have applications as longevity treatments, for end-of-life care, for the treatment and prevention of mental health conditions, and for the betterment of mental functioning and overall health and wellbeing, such as by heightening consciousness and enhancing cognitive function.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS The invention claimed is: 1. A therapeutic âbrain boostâ combination or kit of parts for enhancing energy production, comprising: a. a serotonergic psychedelic; b. a mitochondrial energy enhancer; and c. an antioxidant or glucose regulator. 2. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein: a. the serotonergic psychedelic is lysergic acid diethylamide (LSD); b. the mitochondrial energy enhancer is nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide as NAD+; and c. the antioxidant or glucose regulator is ascorbic acid (vitamin C). 3. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is a tryptamine, a phenethylamine, or a lysergamide. 4. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is any of LSD, 1 P-LSD, AL-LAD, psilocybin, psilocin, psilacetin, mescaline, DMT, DOI, 5-MeO-DMT, and ibogaine. 5. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is LSD. 6. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein the mitochondrial energy enhancer is a metabolic cofactor or a metabolic coenzyme. 7. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein the mitochondrial energy enhancer is a nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD) compound. 8. The combination or kit of parts of claim 7, wherein the NAD compound is any of NAD+, NADH, a-NAD+, a-NADH, P-NAD+, -NADH, and a combination thereof. 9. The combination or kit of parts of claim 8, wherein the NAD compound is NAD+. 10. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1, wherein the mitochondrial energy enhancer is a NAD+ precursor. 11. The combination or kit of parts of claim 10, wherein the NAD+ precursor is any of nicotinic acid (NA), nicotinic acid mononucleotide (NaMN), nicotinic acid adenine dinucleotide (NAAD), nicotinic acid riboside (NaR), nicotinamide (NAM), nicotinamide mononucleotide (NMN), nicotinamide riboside (NR), inositol hexanicotinate (IHN), and tryptophan (Trp). 12. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1, wherein the mitochondrial energy enhancer is any of flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD), coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10), acetyl-L-carnitine (ALC), alpha-lipoic acid (ALA), a B-complex vitamin, and a mineral. 13. The combination or kit of parts of claim 12, wherein the B-complex vitamin is vitamin B1, vitamin B2, 

 vitamin B3, vitamin B5, vitamin B6, vitamin B7, vitamin B9, vitamin B12, or a combination thereof. 14. The combination or kit of parts of claim 12, wherein the mineral is iodine, sulfur, or magnesium. 15. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1, wherein the antioxidant or glucose regulator is ascorbic acid (vitamin C) or coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10). 16. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1, further comprising a NAD+ degradation inhibitor. 17. The combination or kit of parts of claim 16, wherein the NAD+ degradation inhibitor is any of a CD38 inhibitor, a PARP inhibitor, a SIRT inhibitor, and a SARM1 inhibitor. 18. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1, further comprising any of a SIRT activator, an agentthat increases SIRT activity, an agentthat preserves SIRT activity, an agent that enhances SIRT synthesis or protein expression, an agent that enhances the half-life of a SIRT protein, an agent that promotes NADH oxidation, an activator of AMP-activated kinase, an agent that increases expression of nicotinamide phosphoribosyltransferase (PRT), an agent that acts as a substrate for NAD+ synthesis, an agent that lessens the reduction of NAD+ mediated by glycolysis, a methyl-donor supplement, and a NAMPT activating agent. 19. The combination or kit of parts of claim 18, wherein the methyl-donor supplement is trimethylglycine. 20. The combination or kit of parts of claim 18, wherein the NAMPT activating agent is berberine, resveratrol, or P7C3. 21 . The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , further comprising an additional active agent. 22. The combination or kit of parts of claim 21 , wherein the additional active agent is selected from the group consisting of: amino acids, antioxidants, anti-inflammatory agents, analgesics, antineuropathic agents, antinociceptive agents, antimigraine agents, anxiolytics, antidepressants, antipsychotics, anti-PTSD agents, dissociatives, hypnotics, cannabinoids, immunostimulants, anti-cancer agents, antiemetics, orexigenics, antiulcer agents, antihistamines, antihypertensives, anticonvulsants, antiepileptics, bronchodilators, neuroprotectants, nootropics, empathogens, entactogens, psychedelics, plasticity-inducing agents, psychoplastogens, neuroplastogens, monoamine oxidase inhibitors, reversible inhibitors of monoamine oxidase-A (RIMAs), tryptamines, terpenes, phenethylamines, sedatives, stimulants, serotonergic agents, and vitamins. 23. The combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , wherein each of the serotonergic psychedelic, the mitochondrial energy enhancer, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator are separately formulated as a pharmaceutical composition, each further comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, diluent, or excipient. 24. The combination or kit of parts of claim 23, wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is LSD, the mitochondrial energy enhancer is NAD+, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator is vitamin C. 25. The combination or kit of parts of claim 24, wherein LSD is in an amount of between about 1 pg and about 50 pg, between about 5 pg and about 20 pg, less than about 50 pg, less than about 20 pg, less than about 10 pg, or about 10 pg. 26. A pharmaceutical composition for enhancing energy production, comprising: a. a serotonergic psychedelic; b. a mitochondrial energy enhancer; and c. an antioxidant or glucose regulator; and further comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, diluent, or excipient. 27. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, suitable for enteral or parenteral administration. 28. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, suitable for oral administration. 29. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, in unit dosage form. 30. The pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is LSD, the mitochondrial energy enhancer is NAD+, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator is vitamin C. 31 . A method for enhancing energy production in a subject, comprising administering to the subject the combination or kit of parts of claim 1 . 32. The method of claim 31 , wherein administration is for the treatment of a medical condition. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the medical condition is any of a nervous system condition or disorder; a mental, behavioral, or neurodevelopmental disorder; a mental health disorder; a neurodegenerative disorder; a mitochondrial disorder; and a condition related to energy production. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the nervous system condition or disorder is any of addiction, a substance use disorder, a depressive disorder, an anxiety disorder, Alzheimerâs disease, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), an autism spectrum disorder, cluster headaches, migraines, cognitive decline, dementia, an eating disorder, grief, existential distress associated with terminal illness or mortality, Parkinsonâs disease, and post traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). 35. The method of claim 33, wherein the mental, behavioral, or neurodevelopmental disorder is any of a neurodevelopmental disorder, schizophrenia, a primary psychotic disorder, catatonia, a mood disorder, anxiety, a fear-related disorder, an obsessive-compulsive or related disorder, a disorder associated with stress, a dissociative disorder, a feeding or eating disorder, an elimination disorder, a disorder of bodily distress or bodily experience, a disorder due to substance use or an addictive behavior, an impulse control disorder, a disruptive behavior or dissocial disorder, a personality disorder, a paraphilic disorder, a factitious disorder, a neurocognitive disorder, a mental or behavioral disorder associated with pregnancy, childbirth or the puerperium, a sleep-wake disorder, a sexual dysfunction, and gender incongruence. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the neurodevelopmental disorder is any of autism spectrum disorder, cerebral palsy, epilepsy, bipolar disorder, obsessive compulsive disorder, a disorder of communication, speech, or language, fetal alcohol syndrome or fetal alcohol effects (FAS/FAE), fragile X syndrome, Williamâs syndrome, and Down syndrome. 37. The method of claim 33, wherein the mental health disorder is any of disruptive mood dysregulation disorder, major depressive disorder (MDD), treatment resistant depression (TRD), persistent depressive disorder (dysthymia), premenstrual dysphoric disorder, a substance/medication-induced depressive disorder, post-partum depression, a depressive disorder due to another medical condition, separation anxiety disorder, selective mutism, a specific phobia, a social anxiety disorder, social phobia, a panic disorder, panic attacks, agoraphobia, generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), a substance or medication-induced anxiety disorder, an anxiety disorder due to another medical condition, a somatic symptom disorder, an illness anxiety disorder, a conversion disorder, a functional neurological symptom disorder, factitious disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), an adjustment disorder, a trauma or stressor-related disorder, an acute distress disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, body dysmorphic disorder, hoarding disorder, trichotillomania, excoriation, a substance or medication-induced obsessive-compulsive or related disorder, an obsessive-compulsive or related disorder due to another medical condition, a substance-related disorder, an alcohol-related disorder, a cannabis-related disorder, a hallucinogen-related disorder, an inhalant-related disorder, a cocaine-related disorder, an opioid-related disorder, a sedative-, hypnotic-, or anxiolytic-related disorder, a stimulant-related disorder, a tobacco-related disorder, a non-substance-related disorder, a behavioral addiction, migraines, cluster headaches, cyclical vomiting, tension-type headaches, dysphasia, pica, anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, a binge-eating disorder, oppositional defiant disorder, intermittent explosive disorder, a conduct disorder, antisocial personality disorder, psychopathy, pyromania, and kleptomania. 38. The method of claim 33, wherein the neurodegenerative disorder is any of Alzheimerâs disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Charcotâs disease, chronic traumatic encephalopathy, corticobasal degeneration, dementia, vascular dementia, Huntingtonâs disease, Lytico-Bodig disease, mild cognitive impairment, multiple sclerosis, a motor neuron disease, neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder, Parkinsonâs disease, parkinsonisms, a prion disease, progressive supranuclear palsy, traumatic brain injury, and mild traumatic brain injury. 39. The method of claim 33, wherein the mitochondrial disorder is any of myopathy with exercise intolerance, eyelid ptosis, ophthalmoparesis, pigmentary retinopathy, sensorineural hearing loss, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, migraine disorder, mitochondrial disease not otherwise specified, chronic fatigue immunity deficiency syndrome, myalgic encephalomyelitis, chronic fatigue syndrome, demyelination due to mitochondrial disease, mitochondrial cytopathy, a liver disease due to a mitochondrial disorder, a disorder of mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation, a mitochondrial DNA depletion syndrome, an 

 unspecified disorder of mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation, a multiple mitochondrial DNA deletion syndrome, a mitochondrial protein translation defect, a mitochondrial myopathy, a glomerular disease associated with a mitochondrial disease, mitochondrial diabetes, myoclonus due to a mitochondrial cytopathy, a disorder of mitochondrial membrane transport, epilepsy due to a mitochondrial disorder, an intestinal congenital motility disorder due to a mitochondrial disease, mitochondrial ocular myopathy, congenital muscular dystrophy with mitochondrial structural abnormalities, a disorder of mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation, ataxia due to a mitochondrial mutation, mitochondrial isocitrate dehydrogenase deficiency, chorea due to mitochondriopathy, mitochondrial thiamine pyrophosphate carrier deficiency, dystonia due to a mitochondrial cytopathy, dilated cardiomyopathy due to a mitochondrial myopathy, mitochondrial disease with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, mitochondrial myopathy with sideroblastic anemia, and mitochondrial 2-methylacetoacetyl-CoA thiolase deficiency. 40. The method of claim 33, wherein the condition related to energy production is a condition caused by or associated with any of reduced cellular energy metabolism, reduced ATP production, reduced mitochondrial biosynthesis, and reduced NAD, NADH, and/or NAD+ levels. 41 . The method of claim 31 , wherein enhancing energy production in the subject improves mental health and functioning, or improves overall health and wellness. 42. The method of claim 31 , wherein the serotonergic psychedelic, the mitochondrial energy enhancer, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator are administered in separate dosage forms. 43. The method of claim 31 , wherein the serotonergic psychedelic, the mitochondrial energy enhancer, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator are administered separately, sequentially, or simultaneously. 44. The method of claim 31 , wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is LSD, the mitochondrial energy enhancer is NAD+, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator is vitamin C. 45. A method for enhancing energy production in a subject, comprising administering to the subject the pharmaceutical composition of claim 26. 46. The method of claim 45, wherein the serotonergic psychedelic is LSD, the mitochondrial energy enhancer is NAD+, and the antioxidant or glucose regulator is vitamin C. 47. Use of the combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, for enhancing energy production, treating a medical condition, improving mental health and functioning, or improving overall health and wellness. 48. The use of the combination or kit of parts of claim 1 , or the pharmaceutical composition of claim 26, for the manufacture of a medicament for the therapeutic or prophylactic treatment of a medical condition. 49. A therapeutic âbrain boostâ combination or kit of parts for enhancing energy production, comprising a serotonergic psychedelic and a mitochondrial energy enhancer.
